title: Living Meta-Analysis: A CT.gov-Native Browser Application for Continuously Updated Evidence Synthesis
author: Mahmood Ahmad
body: Can a browser application automate the surveillance and analytic refresh cycles required for living systematic reviews of clinical evidence? Living Meta-Analysis is a TypeScript web application providing automated updates from ClinicalTrials.gov, two-stage screening with a rules engine, auto-fill data extraction, and pairwise meta-analysis using four heterogeneity estimators validated against R metafor. The platform implements network meta-analysis with inconsistency testing, trial sequential analysis with O-Brien-Fleming boundaries, robust variance estimation, selection models, and an evidence integrity module detecting outcome reporting bias. All core statistical methods passed validation with tau-squared matching within 0.0001 and prediction intervals using correct degrees-of-freedom specification. The evidence integrity module cross-references registered primary outcomes against published reports to flag selective reporting across the review timeline. This architecture enables clinical teams to maintain continuously updated evidence syntheses without switching between screening, analysis, and reporting tools. However, the limitation of API-only surveillance means studies not registered on ClinicalTrials.gov will be missed without manual supplementation from additional databases.

sentences.text: Can a browser application automate the surveillance and analytic refresh cycles required for living systematic reviews of clinical evidence?

Living Meta-Analysis is a TypeScript web application providing automated updates from ClinicalTrials.gov, two-stage screening with a rules engine, auto-fill data extraction, and pairwise meta-analysis using four heterogeneity estimators validated against R metafor.

The platform implements network meta-analysis with inconsistency testing, trial sequential analysis with O-Brien-Fleming boundaries, robust variance estimation, selection models, and an evidence integrity module detecting outcome reporting bias.
All core statistical methods passed validation with tau-squared matching within 0.0001 and prediction intervals using correct degrees-of-freedom specification.
The evidence integrity module cross-references registered primary outcomes against published reports to flag selective reporting across the review timeline.
This architecture enables clinical teams to maintain continuously updated evidence syntheses without switching between screening, analysis, and reporting tools.
However, the limitation of API-only surveillance means studies not registered on ClinicalTrials.gov will be missed without manual supplementation from additional databases.

primary_estimand: Pooled treatment effect with sequential monitoring

checks.name: single paragraph
checks.detail: Body must not contain blank-line paragraph breaks.

checks.name: no headings
checks.detail: No markdown headings allowed in body.

checks.name: no links
checks.detail: No links or DOI links allowed in body.

checks.name: result sentence has interval
checks.detail: Sentence 4 should include a confidence interval.

checks.name: result sentence has estimand
checks.detail: Sentence 4 should name an effect measure or test metric.

checks.name: boundary sentence present
checks.detail: Sentence 7 should express a limitation, harm, or scope boundary.
